title: Workflow
description: Declarative composition of microskills and agents into a deterministic DAG. Compiled to Claude Code Workflow-JS segments + a run manifest; segments run on the native Workflow engine, the dispatcher orchestrates segments and human checkpoints.

version.description: Schema version. Only 1 is recognized. Omitting it defaults to 1 (set by compile/validate before validation); an explicit value other than 1 still blocks.

base.description: Catalog curation hint: true marks this as a base component that initialize-harness seeds into a new project's harness.yaml. Ignored by the compiler.

profile.description: base.yaml only; names the default profile overlay applied when --profile is omitted.

vars.description: Intra-def variable map. Double-brace {{key}} tokens anywhere in the merged doc are substituted from this map by compile/validate AFTER deep-merge (so a profile may override a var) and BEFORE schema validation. Pure textual pre-compile transform. An unresolved {{key}} is left intact (warns). Cross-file shared prose rides the separate {{snippet:NAME}} token, replaced (BEFORE var substitution, so snippets may carry vars) with the bytes of <defs-root>/_snippets/NAME.md — an unresolved snippet is a HARD block. V1: _snippets/ is plugin/engine-owned, vendored by initialize-harness only.

imports.description: Load-bearing child-workflow allowlist for `workflow:` nodes: every bare child-workflow name a `workflow:` node may invoke MUST be listed here (used by validate-workflow for nested-target resolution + import-cycle detection). A legacy `<dir>/<name>` advisory form is still accepted for un-migrated defs but is ignored by the nested-resolution pass.

materialize.description: Marks this as a large / multi-shape input passed BY REFERENCE. The dispatcher normalizes the caller-supplied value — a raw string, a single file path, or a directory of files — into ONE canonical file on disk and threads only that short path into the segment args (the consuming microskill body reads it). Avoids the native-engine args-size truncation that drops large inline values. The input name should end in `_path`. Only `file` is defined today (room for `dir` later).

nodes.description: DAG nodes. Each is a leaf microskill (use:) or an agent invocation (agent:). NOTE: the static fan-out sugar — a template node's `expand: {over: [...]}` and a consumer's `inputs_each: <template-id>` — is desugared by compile/validate BEFORE this schema runs (generating ordinary <id>_<item> sibling nodes and the fan-in inputs/depends_on), so it is intentionally NOT part of this closed grammar; the schema only ever sees the generated nodes.

id.description: Unique node id within the workflow.

agent.description: Agent type to invoke for this node (e.g. microskill-planner).

workflow.description: Bare child-workflow name (kebab-case) invoked as an orchestrator checkpoint. Must be listed in the top-level `imports` allowlist. Mutually exclusive with use:/agent:; carries inputs/depends_on/when/for_each/as.

depends_on.description: Upstream node ids. Every ${<id>.output...} reference MUST be declared here.

when.description: Deterministic JS condition (inner of ${...}); false → node skipped.

for_each.description: Expression resolving to a collection → fan-out one instance per item.

as.description: Loop-variable name bound to each for_each item; referenced as ${<as>}.

inputs.description: Wiring: input field → ${...} expression (workflow inputs, upstream outputs, loop carry, for_each item).

prompt.description: For agent nodes: the task prompt template (may contain ${...} references).

output_schema.description: JSON Schema fragment forcing structured node output (passed to agent() schema). For a use: node, OMITTING this inherits the resolved microskill's output_schema; an explicit value here wins.

max_parallel.description: Only valid on a for_each node: bound the fan-out to batches of this size (parallelChunked) instead of one unbounded parallel().

phase_group.description: Cosmetic /workflows progress-group label: nodes sharing a phase_group render under one group box. Emit-only — changes the segment's phase markers / meta.phases, never the manifest or manifest_hash.

customize.description: Per-node knobs, CLOSED to {profile?, overrides?} — an unknown key fails schema validation (it used to be silently ignored).

profile.description: On a use: node, selects which profile of the microskill the node runs; on a workflow: node, selects the child workflow's profile (passed through to the nested-workflow checkpoint).

overrides.description: use: nodes ONLY (a hard error on workflow:/agent:/orchestrator nodes, where it could never apply). Flat map of dotted resolver-config path -> value (e.g. 'runtime.model': haiku), applied to the microskill's resolution exactly like `resolve-microskill --override` (SET at the path — not a deep-merge). Plumbed through BOTH resolution paths so they cannot diverge: the classify-time resolution (an override exposing AskUserQuestion deterministically reclassifies the node to an orchestrator checkpoint) and the frozen payload the baked runMicroskill reads (plus the execution-time --inject-only command line, shell-escaped). Recorded in the manifest as node_overrides only when non-empty, so override-free compiles stay byte-identical. CLI twin: `compile-workflow --node-override <node-id>:<dotted>=<value>`.

retry.description: Bounded retry for a BACKGROUND use:/agent: node only (a block anywhere else — retry lives in compiled segment JS and cannot wrap an orchestrator checkpoint). The node's call is wrapped in withRetry(...): a thrown rejection AND a null/undefined return both count as a failed attempt (host contract: a failed agent() call in the native engine typically RETURNS NULL rather than throwing); after exhausting max_attempts the FINAL attempt's native failure shape is reproduced exactly (return null / rethrow) — never re-rolled, never fabricated. The helper is emitted only into segments that contain a retry node, so retry-free defs compile byte-identically.

max_attempts.description: Total attempt cap, first try included (boundedness mirrors loop.max_iters).

grant_tools.description: Extra tools granted to this node's subagent.

delegation.description: Override the auto-derived delegation classification. `orchestrator` forces a checkpoint (on a use: node it also skips resolution — the escape hatch for an unresolvable target). `auto` forces background only for a node that can actually run there: a use: node still resolves (executor identity is stashed) and the compile dies if the resolved microskill exposes AskUserQuestion or carries a hard gate; `auto` on a workflow: node or an orchestrator-native step is a hard error.

side_effect.description: Readable alias for delegation: orchestrator. true normalizes to delegation: orchestrator before classification, so the node runs as an orchestrator checkpoint (where filesystem side effects and human interaction are allowed). Pairing it with delegation: auto is a contradiction — hard error in compile and validate.

gates.description: Checkpoints anchored after a node. human_approval / hard-severity gates run in the orchestrator (segment boundaries).

after.description: Node id this gate fires after.

options.description: Choices for a human_approval gate (e.g. approve/revise/abandon).

loop.description: Guarded loop over a subset of nodes. Compiled to a JS while/do-while inside a single autonomous segment. Exactly one of while/until must be present (enforced by compile/validate, not the schema).

while.description: Continue-condition (inner of ${...}) over the latest iteration's output. Mutually exclusive with until.

until.description: Stop-condition sugar (inner of ${...}): until: EXPR desugars to while: !(EXPR). Mutually exclusive with while.

body.description: Node ids that repeat each iteration.

carry.description: State passed to the next iteration: var → ${...} expression.

from.description: Node id whose output is the workflow's aggregate output.
